title: Integrated circuits having reduced dimensions between components
abstract: In a particular aspect, an integrated circuit includes a first transistor including a first source region and a first drain region. The integrated circuit includes a second transistor including a second source region and a second drain region. The integrated circuit includes a first gate structure coupled to the first transistor and to the second transistor. The first gate structure is included in a first layer. The integrated circuit further includes a first metal line coupled to the first source region and to the second drain region. The first metal line has a two-dimensional routing arrangement and is included in a second layer that is distinct from the first layer.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS; 1. An integrated circuit comprising: a first transistor including a first source region and a first drain region; a second transistor including a second source region and a second drain region; a first gate structure coupled to the first transistor and to the second transistor, wherein the first gate structure is included in a first layer; and a first metal line coupled to the first source region and to the second drain region, the first metal line having a two-dimensional routing arrangement, wherein the first metal line is included in a second layer that is distinct from the first layer. 2. The integrated circuit of claim 1, wherein a first pitch between the first gate structure and an adjacent gate structure is greater than or equal to approximately 80 nanometers (nm) and less than approximately 100 nm, and wherein a second pitch between the first metal line and an adjacent metal line is approximately 90 nm. 3. The integrated circuit of claim 1, wherein the first transistor and the second transistor comprise fin field-effect transistor (FinFET) devices, and wherein the second layer comprises a metal-1 layer. 4. The integrated circuit of claim 1, wherein the first gate structure is oriented along an axis in a first direction, wherein a first portion of the first metal line is routed in the first direction, and wherein a second portion of the first metal line is routed in a second direction substantially perpendicular to the first direction. 5. The integrated circuit of claim 1, further comprising: a first contact structure coupled to the first source region and coupled by a first via structure to the first metal line; and a second contact structure coupled to the second drain region and coupled by a second via structure to the first metal line, wherein the first contact structure and the second contact structure are included in the first layer. 6. The integrated circuit of claim 5, wherein the first layer comprises a metal-0 layer. 7. The integrated circuit of claim 5, further comprising: a second gate structure; a third contact structure coupled to the first gate structure; and a second metal line coupled by a third via structure to the third contact structure. 8. The integrated circuit of claim 1 , wherein the first transistor and the second transistor are included in a cell, wherein the first gate structure is oriented along an axis in a first direction, and wherein a first dimension of the cell in the first direction substantially exceeds a second dimension of the cell in a second direction that is perpendicular to the first direction. 9. The integrated circuit of claim 1 , wherein the first transistor and the second transistor are included in a communications device, a personal digital assistant (PDA), a mobile phone, a cellular phone, a navigation device, a computer, a portable computer, a desktop computer, a set top box, an entertainment unit, a fixed location data unit, a mobile location data unit, a monitor, a computer monitor, a television, a tuner, a radio, a satellite radio, a music player, a digital music player, a portable music player, a video player, a digital video player, a digital video disc (DVD) player, a portable digital video player, or a combination thereof. 10. An integrated circuit comprising: a first transistor including a first source region and a first drain region; a second transistor including a second source region and a second drain region; a first gate structure coupled to the first transistor and to the second transistor; a first contact structure coupled to the first gate structure; a second contact structure coupled to the first source region and to the second drain region, wherein the first gate structure and the second contact structure are included in a first layer; a first metal line coupled to the first contact structure; and 

 a second metal line coupled to the second contact structure, wherein the first metal line and the second metal line are included in a second layer that is distinct from the first layer, wherein the first gate structure is oriented along an axis in a first direction, and wherein the first metal line and the second metal line are routed in a second direction that is substantially perpendicular to the first direction. 11. The integrated circuit of claim 10, wherein the first contact structure is coupled by a first via structure to the first metal line, wherein the second contact structure is coupled by a second via structure to the second metal line, and wherein a via pitch between the first via structure and the second via structure is greater than or equal to approximately 90 nanometers (nm). 12. The integrated circuit of claim 10, further comprising a second gate structure, wherein a first pitch between the first gate structure and the second gate structure is between approximately 100 nanometers (nm) and approximately 120 nm, and wherein a second pitch between the first metal line and an adjacent metal line is less than approximately 64 nm. 13. The integrated circuit of claim 12, wherein the second pitch is within a range between approximately 48 nm and approximately 52 nm. 14. The integrated circuit of claim 10, wherein the first layer comprises a metal- 0 layer, and wherein the second layer comprises a metal-1 layer. 15. The integrated circuit of claim 10, wherein the first metal line and the second metal line have a one-dimensional routing arrangement. 16. The integrated circuit of claim 10, wherein the first transistor and the second transistor comprise fin field-effect transistor (FinFET) devices. 17. The integrated circuit of claim 10, wherein the first transistor and the second transistor are included in a cell, wherein a second dimension of the cell in the second direction substantially exceeds a first dimension of the cell in the first direction. 18. A method of fabricating an integrated circuit, the method comprising: forming a first gate structure in a first layer above an active area layer of a first transistor and a second transistor, the first transistor including a first source region and a first drain region, the second transistor including a second source region and a second drain region; and forming, in a second layer that is distinct from the first layer, a first metal line coupled to the first source region and to the second drain region, the first metal line having a two-dimensional routing arrangement. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising forming a plurality of metal lines in the second layer using a single patterning process, the plurality of metal lines including the first metal line. 20. The method of claim 18, wherein the first gate structure is formed using a high-k/metal gate (HK/MG) last process. 21. The method of claim 18, further comprising: forming a second gate structure, wherein a first pitch between the first gate structure and the second gate structure is greater than or equal to approximately 80 nanometers (nm) and less than approximately 100 nm; and forming a second metal line adjacent to the first metal line, wherein a second pitch between the first metal line and the second metal line is approximately 90 nm. 22. The method of claim 18, further comprising: forming a first contact structure coupled to the first source region; forming a second contact structure coupled to the second drain region; and forming a third contact structure coupled to the first gate structure, wherein the first contact structure, the second contact structure, and the third contact structure are formed during a middle of line (MOL) fabrication stage. 23. The method of claim 18, further comprising forming, in a third layer that is distinct from the second layer, a second plurality of metal lines using a single patterning process. 24. The method of claim 18, wherein the first transistor and the second transistor comprise fin field-effect transistor (FinFET) devices. 25. A method of fabricating an integrated circuit, the method comprising: forming a first gate structure in a first layer above an active area layer of a first transistor and a second transistor, the first transistor including a first source region and a first drain region, the second transistor including a second source region and a second drain region; forming, in the first layer, a first contact structure coupled to the first gate structure; forming, in the first layer, a second contact structure coupled to the first source region and to the second drain region; forming, in a second layer that is distinct from the first layer, a first metal line coupled to the first contact structure, wherein the first gate structure is oriented along an axis in a first direction, and wherein the first metal line is routed in a second direction that is substantially perpendicular to the first direction; and forming, in the second layer, a second metal line coupled to the second contact structure and routed in the second direction. 26. The method of claim 25, further comprising forming a plurality of metal lines in the second layer using a double patterning process, the plurality of metal lines including the first metal line and the second metal line. 27. The method of claim 25, further comprising forming a first via structure and a second via structure using a single patterning process, wherein the first contact structure is coupled by the first via structure to the first metal line, and wherein the second contact structure is coupled by the second via structure to the second metal line. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein a via pitch between the first via structure and the second via structure is greater than or equal to approximately 90 nanometers (nm). 29. The method of claim 25, further comprising: forming a second gate structure, wherein a first pitch between the first gate structure and the second gate structure is between approximately 100 nanometers (nm) and approximately 120 nm; and forming a third metal line between the first metal line and the second metal line, wherein a second pitch between the first metal line and the third metal line is less than approximately 64 nm. 30. The method of claim 25, wherein the first gate structure is formed using a high-k/metal gate (HK/MG) last process.
